zh.lifeSummary: 當George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus於1376年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾諾斯 貝維其可時，他的父親Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar54歲，母親Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus23歲。他於1397年5月24日與Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1402年9月14日在英國享年26歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus于1376年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾諾斯 貝維其可时，他的父亲Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar54岁，母亲Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus23岁。他于1397年5月24日与Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1402年9月14日在英国享年26岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus was born in 1376, in North Berwick, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, his father, Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar, was 54 and his mother, Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus, was 23. He married Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus on 24 May 1397, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 14 September 1402, in England, at the age of 26, and was buried in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus est né en 1376, à North Berwick, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus le 24 mai 1397, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1402, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 26 ans, et a été enterré à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

Er hat am 24. Mai 1397 in Scotland Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 14. ja.lifeSummary: George Douglas 1st Earl of Angusが1376年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアノース バーウィックで生まれた時，父Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Marは54歳，母Margaret Stewart Countess of Angusは23歳であった。1397年5月24日にスコットランドでPrincess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angusと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1402年9月14日にイギリスで26歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus(이)가 1376년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 노르스 버윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1397년 5월 24일 스컷랜드에서 Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1402년 9월 14일 영국에서 26세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus em 24 de maio de 1397, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Douglas 1st Earl of Angus родился в 1376 г. в местности норт бирвикк, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Stewart Countess of Angus, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Mary Stewart Stewart Countess Angus 24 мая 1397 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 14 сентября 1402 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 26 лет и был похоронен в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия.
